node.statement: (Applying the main bound). Under m affine constraints φ(f_j) = ∫ f_j dμ = β_j for f_j ∈ C(X, R): Σ_{j=1}^k n_j² = k·1 = k ≤ m + 1. This says: extreme points of the constrained set of probability measures are discrete measures supported on at most m+1 points. This is precisely the Richter-Rogosinski theorem (the measure-theoretic form of Carathéodory's theorem for moment problems).
